commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: Approvals: add Telegram admin terminal routing flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: approval requests could be duplicated or routed inconsistently when using explicit approval targets (especially Telegram DM admin-terminal setups).
- Why it matters: operator UX became noisy/confusing, and forwarding behavior could conflict with origin-channel messaging.
- What changed: approval routing, surface selection, and Telegram approval handling were tightened so `targets` mode behaves predictably, forwarding stays generic, and auth checks remain explicit.
- What did NOT change: no trust broadening, no hardcoded personal routing, no local deployment secrets/config in code.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: approval delivery selection and origin-surface behavior were evaluated in separate places without a single consistent policy for `targets` mode + turn-source availability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: integration coverage for explicit-target routing plus origin-surface interaction was incomplete.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): N/A
- Why this regressed now: admin-terminal routing introduced legitimate new paths that exposed inconsistent edge handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/exec-approvals.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/exec-approvals-handler.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: explicit-target forwarding, origin-route availability behavior, Telegram approver/account resolution, and pending-message text consistency.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these files exercise the exact routing and auth seams where duplication/misrouting occurred.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): extended in-place.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- In `approvals.*.mode = "targets"`, approval routing now favors configured targets without sending redundant origin approval prompts.
- Telegram explicit-target handling remains DM-first and approver-restricted.
- Origin channels receive cleaner pending state behavior when admin-terminal forwarding owns routing.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[action in origin chat] -> [forwarding + origin client broadcast overlap] -> [duplicate/noisy approval surfaces]

After:
[action in origin chat] -> [compute route + turn-source availability] -> [target-only forwarding OR origin surface] -> [single coherent approval path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local dev
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram + cross-channel forwarding surfaces
- Relevant config (redacted): `approvals.exec/plugin.mode=targets` with Telegram targets and Telegram approvers

### Steps

1. Trigger exec/plugin approval from an origin channel with explicit Telegram target configured.
2. Observe delivery and pending state behavior in origin + target surfaces.
3. Approve/deny from Telegram and verify single resolution lifecycle.

### Expected

- One coherent approval path, no duplicate actionable prompts, approver checks enforced.

### Actual

- Verified in updated tests and manual validation paths.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: explicit target routing for exec/plugin approvals, Telegram approver behavior, pending/resolve lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: origin route present vs absent, targets-only mode, non-Telegram origin to Telegram target.
- What you did **not** verify: full mobile UX capture in this PR thread.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: route-selection behavior changes could miss an edge channel path.
  - Mitigation: added/expanded forwarder + gateway + Telegram tests and preserved fail-closed approval semantics.
